pages.0:
## Page 1

HC-05 Bluetooth module  
iteadstudio.com 
06.18.2010 
1 
     Tech Support: info@iteadstudio.com 
HC-05 
-Bluetooth to Serial Port Module  
Overview 
 
HC-05 module is an easy to use Bluetooth SPP (Serial Port Protocol) module, designed for 
transparent wireless serial connection setup.  
Serial port Bluetooth module is fully qualified Bluetooth V2.0+EDR (Enhanced Data Rate) 3Mbps 
Modulation with complete 2.4GHz radio transceiver and baseband. It uses CSR Bluecore 
04-External single chip Bluetooth system with CMOS technology and with AFH(Adaptive 
Frequency Hopping Feature). It has the footprint as small as 12.7mmx27mm. Hope it will simplify 
your overall design/development cycle. 
Specifications 
Hardware features  
 
Typical -80dBm sensitivity  
 
Up to +4dBm RF transmit power   
 
Low Power 1.8V Operation ,1.8 to 3.6V I/O  
 
PIO control 
 
UART interface with programmable baud rate  
 
With integrated antenna  
 
With edge connector

**Embedded Image 1**

```json
{
  "sections": []
}
```
pages.1:
## Page 2

HC-05 Bluetooth module  
iteadstudio.com 
06.18.2010 
2 
     Tech Support: info@iteadstudio.com 
  
Software features  
 
Default Baud rate: 38400, Data bits:8, Stop bit:1,Parity:No parity, Data control: has. 
Supported baud rate: 9600,19200,38400,57600,115200,230400,460800.  
 
Given a rising pulse in PIO0, device will be disconnected.  
 
Status instruction port PIO1: low-disconnected, high-connected;  
 
PIO10 and PIO11 can be connected to red and blue led separately. When master and slave 
are paired, red and blue led blinks 1time/2s in interval, while disconnected only blue led 
blinks 2times/s.  
 
Auto-connect to the last device on power as default.  
 
Permit pairing device to connect as default.  
 
Auto-pairing PINCODE:”0000” as default  
 
Auto-reconnect in 30 min when disconnected as a result of beyond the range of connection. 
 
 
Hardware

## Page 5

HC-05 Bluetooth module  
iteadstudio.com 
06.18.2010 
5 
     Tech Support: info@iteadstudio.com 
 
AT command Default: 
 How to set the mode to server (master):  
1. Connect PIO11 to high level. 
2. Power on, module into command state. 
3. Using baud rate 38400, sent the “AT+ROLE=1\r\n” to module, with “OK\r\n” 
means setting successes. 
4. Connect the PIO11 to low level, repower the module, the module work as server 
(master). 
  
AT commands: (all end with \r\n)  
1. Test command: 
Command 
Respond 
Parameter 
AT 
OK 
- 
2. Reset 
Command 
Respond 
Parameter 
AT+RESET 
OK 
- 
 
3. Get firmware version 
Command 
Respond 
Parameter 
AT+VERSION? 
+VERSION:<Param> 
OK 
Param : firmware version 
Example:  
AT+VERSION?\r\n  
+VERSION:2.0-20100601  
OK

```json
  "sections": [
```

```json
      "heading": "9. Set/Check device class",

      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+CLASS=<Param>",
        "Respond": "OK",
        "Parameter": "Device Class"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "10. Set/Check GIAC (General Inquire Access Code)",
      "content_summary": "This section details commands for setting or checking the General Inquiry Access Code (GIAC). The command AT+IAC=<Param> sets the GIAC, while AT+IAC queries it. Responses are OK or FAIL for set commands, and +IAC:<Param> followed by OK for query. The default GIAC is 9e8b33. An example shows setting GIAC to 9e8b3f and querying it.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+IAC=<Param>",
        "Respond": "1.OK\n2.FAIL",
        "Parameter": "GIAC (Default : 9e8b33)",
        "Example_command_set": "AT+IAC=9e8b3f\\r\\n",
        "Example_response_set": "OK",
        "Example_command_query": "AT+IAC?\\r\\n",
        "Example_response_query": "+IAC: 9e8b3f\nOK"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "11. Set/Check -- Query access patterns",
      "content_summary": "This section outlines commands for configuring inquiry modes and parameters. The command AT+INQM=<Param>,<Param2>,<Param3> sets the inquiry mode, maximum number of responding devices, and timeout. AT+INQM? queries these settings. Parameters include inquiry_mode_standard (0) or inquiry_mode_rssi (1), Param2 for max devices, and Param3 for timeout (1-48, corresponding to 1.28s to 61.44s). An example sets mode to 1, max devices to 9, and timeout to 48.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+INQM=<Param>,<Param2>,<Param3>",
        "Respond": "1.OK\n2.FAIL",
        "Parameter": "Param: 0—inquiry_mode_standard\n1—inquiry_mode_rssi\nParam2: Maximum number of Bluetooth devices to respond to\nParam3: Timeout (1-48 : 1.28s to 61.44s)",
        "Example_command_set": "AT+INQM=1,9,48\\r\\n",
        "Example_response_set": "OK",
        "Example_command_query": "AT+INQM\\r\\n",
        "Example_response_query": "+INQM:1, 9, 48\nOK"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
      "heading": "Example",
      "content_summary": "Example command: AT+PMSAD=1234,56,abcdef\\r\\n, with response OK.",
      "key_values": {
        "Example Command": "AT+PMSAD=1234,56,abcdef\\r\\n",
        "Example Response": "OK"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "22. Delete All Authenticated Device",
      "content_summary": "Command to delete all authenticated devices. The command is AT+RMAAD, with response 'OK'. No parameters required.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+RMAAD",
        "Respond": "OK",
        "Parameter": "-"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "23. Search Authenticated Device",
      "content_summary": "Command to search for an authenticated device by its address. The command is AT+FSAD=<Param>, with responses 'OK' or 'FAIL'. Parameter is the device address.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+FSAD=<Param>",
        "Respond": "1. OK\n2. FAIL",
        "Parameter": "Device address"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "24. Get Authenticated Device Count",
      "content_summary": "Command to retrieve the count of authenticated devices. The command is AT+ADCN?, with response '+ADCN: <Param>' followed by 'OK'. Parameter is the device count.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+ADCN?",
        "Respond": "+ADCN: <Param>\nOK",
        "Parameter": "Device Count"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "25. Most Recently Used Authenticated Device",
      "content_summary": "Command to get the address of the most recently used authenticated device. The command is AT+MRAD?, with response '+MRAD: <Param>' followed by 'OK'. Parameter is the recently authenticated device address.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+MRAD?",
        "Respond": "+MRAD: <Param>\nOK",
        "Parameter": "Recently Authenticated Device Address"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "26. Get the module working state",
      "content_summary": "Section heading for retrieving the module's current working state. Table headers are 'Command', 'Respond', and 'Parameter'. No content below the table headers is visible on this page.",
      "key_values": {}
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "sections": [
    {
      "heading": "AT+ STATE?",
      "content_summary": "This section describes the AT command to query the current state of the Bluetooth module, with possible responses including various states like INITIALIZED, READY, PAIRABLE, etc.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+ STATE?",
        "Respond": "+ STATE: <Param>\nOK",
        "Param": "\"INITIALIZED\"\n\"READY\"\n\"PAIRABLE\"\n\"PAIRED\"\n\"INQUIRING\"\n\"CONNECTING\"\n\"CONNECTED\"\n\"DISCONNECTED\"\n\"NUKNOW\""
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "27. Initialize the SPP profile lib",
      "content_summary": "This section details the AT+INIT command used to initialize the Serial Port Profile (SPP) library, with expected responses of OK or FAIL.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+INIT",
        "Respond": "1. OK\n2. FAIL",
        "Parameter": "-"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "28. Inquiry Bluetooth Device",
      "content_summary": "This section explains the AT+INQ command for inquiring nearby Bluetooth devices, returning parameters such as address, device class, and RSSI signal strength. An example sequence is provided showing command inputs and outputs.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+INQ",
        "Respond": "+INQ: <Param1>, <Param2>, <Param3>\n...\nOK",
        "Parameter": "Param1: Address\nParam2: Device Class\nParam3 : RSSI Signal strength"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "Example",
      "content_summary": "A practical example demonstrating the sequence of commands to initiate inquiry, including setting IAC, class, inquiry mode, and then executing the inquiry command. The response lists multiple discovered devices with their addresses, classes, and RSSI values.",
      "key_values": {
        "Commands": "AT+INIT\\r\\n\nOK\nAT+IAC=9e8b33\\r\\n\nOK\nAT+CLASS=0\\r\\n\nAT+INQM=1,9,48\\r\\n\nAt+INQ\\r\\n\n+INQ:2:72:D2224,3E0104,FFBC\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFC1\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFC0\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFC1\n+INQ:2:72:D2224,3F0104,FFAD\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFBE\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFC2\n+INQ:1234:56:0,1F1F,FFBE\n+INQ:2:72:D2224,3F0104,FFBC\nOK"
      }
    },
    {
      "heading": "28. Cancel Inquiring Bluetooth Device",
      "content_summary": "This section describes the AT+ INQC command used to cancel an ongoing Bluetooth device inquiry process.",
      "key_values": {
        "Command": "AT+ INQC",
        "Respond": "OK",
        "Parameter": "-"
```
